title: Shadows and Structures
subtitle: State Secrecy, Bureaucratic Power, and the Limits of Public Knowledge
author: A. Zhou

price.rationale: Entry-level pricing for first book to build reviews. Nonfiction political science. $4.99 ebook hits 70% royalty bracket ($3.49/sale). Paperback at $14.99 for credibility.

description:
What if the most powerful systems in government aren't secret conspiracies—but routine procedures hiding in plain sight?

Shadows and Structures cuts through both naive trust and paranoid speculation to reveal how modern democratic states actually manage secrecy, bureaucratic power, and public knowledge. Drawing on declassification archives, oversight hearings, and administrative law, this book maps the real mechanisms—classification systems, interagency coordination, media incentive structures—that shape what citizens can and cannot know.

This isn't conspiracy theory. It's institutional analysis.

You'll learn:
• How classification systems create durable information asymmetries
• Why well-designed oversight mechanisms degrade over time
• The structural reasons conspiracy narratives persist
• What reform efforts actually achieve—and where they fall short
• How to think clearly about government secrecy without paranoia or naivety

For anyone frustrated by simplistic "trust the government" or "everything is a conspiracy" narratives, this book offers a rigorous middle path grounded in evidence and institutional design.
editing_notes: Manuscript has repetitive paragraphs (AI generation artifacts), [CITATION] placeholders need removal or filling, and some sections repeat the same ideas 3-4 times. Needs a thorough edit pass before publishing. Estimate 4-6 hours of editing work.
